task: Tell me information about what identification I need to bring on my trip on Amtrak.
graderReasoning: The agent completed the task by navigating to Amtrak’s official Passenger Identification page and extracting the relevant identification requirements. The trajectory shows the correct page title and URL (https://www.amtrak.com/passenger-identification) with the needed details visible: customers 18+ must produce valid photo ID in specified situations; valid ID can be one government-issued photo ID or two IDs with at least one government-issued; examples include driver’s license, passport, government-issued ID, military ID, student ID, and Job Corps ID. The agent also correctly included the onboard ticket purchase rule and random ID checks, and appropriately added border-crossing information from Amtrak’s official US-Canada border page. The final response accurately summarizes the official information requested.
final_result_response:
I checked Amtrak’s Passenger Identification page.

For a regular Amtrak trip, bring a **current, valid photo ID** if you’re **18 or older**. Amtrak says you must show it when asked onboard, by Amtrak police/law enforcement, when checking or storing baggage, reprinting/refunding tickets, traveling into Canada, and some other situations. <citation refs="fetch_call_RmIvUwVIvStLTqU9YO6GMdoy|fc_0ec13fdb33f0828e016a0444711b9c8198b33ad0481989eca4#1">“Amtrak customers 18 years of age and older must produce valid photo identification when… Asked onboard trains… Asked any time by Amtrak police… Traveling into Canada.”</citation>

Acceptable ID can be either:

- **One government-issued photo ID**, or
- **Two IDs**, with at least one issued by a government authority.

Examples Amtrak lists include:

- Driver’s license
- Passport
- Government-issued ID
- Military photo ID
- Student photo ID
- Job Corps photo ID <citation refs="fetch_call_RmIvUwVIvStLTqU9YO6GMdoy|fc_0ec13fdb33f0828e016a0444711b9c8198b33ad0481989eca4#1">“Examples of acceptable forms of ID include: State or provincial driver's license; Passport; Official government-issued identification… Military photo ID; Student identification…”</citation>

Also: if you buy a ticket onboard from a conductor, you must show photo ID and be at least 16. Amtrak may do random ticket/ID checks, so keep your ID handy. <citation refs="fetch_call_RmIvUwVIvStLTqU9YO6GMdoy|fc_0ec13fdb33f0828e016a0444711b9c8198b33ad0481989eca4#1">“Please be prepared to show valid photo identification… Passengers purchasing tickets onboard trains from conductors must provide photo identification and be at least 16 years old.”</citation>

If your trip crosses the **U.S.-Canada border**, the rules are stricter: you need the **original, valid ID document** you entered when booking, not a copy, and each passenger needs their own ID. <citation refs="fetch_call_k0hqeqOvNum0SV8zedIYDObB|fc_0ec13fdb33f0828e016a04446ad3e0819883c57d14f591af7f#1">“You must carry the original, valid identification document. Copies, expired or otherwise invalid documents will not be accepted. Each passenger must have their own ID.”</citation>
action_history: Opening Amtrak website: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.amtrak.com/');
